skill.name: scala-pro
skill.description: Master enterprise-grade Scala development with functional programming, distributed systems, and big data processing. Expert in Apache Pekko, Akka, Spark, ZIO/Cats Effect, and reactive architectures. Use PROACTIVELY for Scala system design, performance optimization, or enterprise integration.
skill.summary: Expert Scala developer for enterprise functional programming, distributed systems, and big data applications
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill contains only natural language instructions for Scala development guidance. No suspicious patterns, command execution, network requests, or prompt injection attempts detected. Risk score: 0/100.

content.value_statement: Developers building Scala applications need expert guidance on functional programming patterns, distributed system architecture, and performance optimization. This skill provides enterprise-grade Scala development assistance for Akka, Spark, ZIO, and reactive architectures.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement enterprise Scala applications using functional programming patterns
Build distributed systems with Apache Pekko, Akka, and reactive streaming
Optimize Scala code performance with JVM tuning, profiling, and benchmarking
Implement domain-driven design patterns with algebraic data types and type-safe DSLs
Create type-safe REST and GraphQL APIs with Http4s, Tapir, and Play Framework
Develop big data pipelines using Apache Spark with DataFrame and RDD operations
limitations: Does not execute or compile Scala code - provides guidance only
Cannot access external databases or runtime environments for testing
Limited to Scala ecosystem - does not cover Java, Kotlin, or other JVM languages
Cannot build or deploy applications - only provides architectural advice

use_cases.title: Architecting Distributed Systems
use_cases.description: Design scalable microservices using Akka/Pekko cluster, event sourcing, and CQRS patterns for high-throughput applications
use_cases.target_user: Software architects and senior developers building distributed Scala applications

use_cases.title: Functional Programming Guidance
use_cases.description: Learn best practices for effect systems (ZIO, Cats Effect), type-level programming, and pure functional design
use_cases.target_user: Intermediate developers learning functional programming in Scala

use_cases.title: Performance Optimization
use_cases.description: Optimize JVM performance through profiling, memory management, and GraalVM native image compilation
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers and performance specialists working with Scala applications

prompt_templates.title: Basic Scala Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I implement a safe error handling pattern in Scala using Either or ZIO?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need help with a specific Scala concept or pattern

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Scala microservices design. I have services that need to communicate via gRPC and handle eventual consistency. What patterns should I use?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need architectural guidance for distributed systems

prompt_templates.title: Code Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: My Scala application has slow performance. It processes large datasets with Spark. What profiling tools and optimization strategies should I use?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need help optimizing Scala application performance

prompt_templates.title: Framework Selection
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a type-safe REST API with Scala. Compare Play Framework, Pekko HTTP, and Http4s for my use case with high concurrency requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need help choosing the right Scala framework

output_examples.input: How do I implement a circuit breaker pattern in my Scala Akka application?
output_examples.output: Here's how to implement a circuit breaker using Pekko Resilience: First, add the Pekko Resilience library to your build.sbt. Then create a CircuitBreaker instance with configurable failure threshold and reset timeout. Wrap your external service calls with the circuit breaker to prevent cascade failures. Monitor the circuit state to trigger fallback behaviors when the circuit opens.

output_examples.input: What's the best way to structure a functional Scala project with ZIO?
output_examples.output: Structure your ZIO application with clear separation: Define your business logic as pure functions returning ZIO[R, E, A]. Create a layer of dependencies using ZLayer for database connections, HTTP clients, and configuration. Use the service pattern to compose smaller services into larger applications. Keep the effectful code at the boundary of your application, with pure functions in the core domain.

best_practices: Prefer immutable data structures and pure functions to maximize testability and reasoning about code behavior
Use type-driven development - leverage Scala's type system to enforce correctness at compile time
Separate effectful code from business logic using ZIO or Cats Effect layers for clean architecture
anti_patterns: Avoid mixing imperative and functional code - stick to one paradigm within each module
Do not use var or mutable collections in core business logic - use persistent data structures instead
Avoid catching generic Exceptions - always model errors explicitly with sum types or error channels

faq.question: What is the difference between ZIO and Cats Effect?
faq.answer: Both are functional effect systems for Scala. ZIO provides a built-in error channel and dependency injection via ZLayer. Cats Effect is more lightweight and integrates with Cats libraries. Choose ZIO for integrated error handling or Cats Effect for maximum library compatibility.

faq.question: When should I use Akka/Pekko versus Spark?
faq.answer: Use Akka/Pekko for building distributed applications with the Actor model, real-time streaming, and microservices. Use Apache Spark for batch processing, ETL pipelines, and big data analytics. They serve different purposes and can be used together.

faq.question: How do I migrate from Akka to Apache Pekko?
faq.answer: Apache Pekko is the open-source continuation of Akka. Migration involves updating dependencies from com.typesafe.akka to org.apache.pekko. Most APIs are compatible. Review the Pekko migration guide for breaking changes and use the Pekko Migration Kit for automated help.

faq.question: What are the best testing frameworks for Scala?
faq.answer: ScalaTest and Specs2 are the main testing frameworks. Use ScalaTest with FunSpec or FlatSpec for flexibility. ScalaCheck enables property-based testing. For effect systems, usezio-test and cats-effect-testkit for testing ZIO and Cats Effect code.

faq.question: How do I achieve type-safe database access in Scala?
faq.answer: Use Doobie for pure functional JDBC access, Slick for ORM-style queries, or Quill for compile-time verified SQL. Doobie provides the most control, Slick offers familiar ORM patterns, and Quill generates queries at compile time for maximum safety.

faq.question: What is the best build tool for Scala projects?
faq.answer: SBT is the most common and mature build tool with excellent plugin ecosystem. Mill is gaining popularity for faster builds and simpler configuration. Choose SBT for complex multi-module projects or Mill for faster iteration times.
